title: Prevent JSON injection vulnerabilities
description: JSON operations must be implemented securely to prevent injection attacks. Unsafely handling user input in JSON serialization or deserialization can allow attackers to inject malicious data, leading to security breaches. Always validate and sanitize input before processing JSON.
recommendation: Validate and sanitize all user input before using it in JSON operations. Use secure libraries and avoid direct string concatenation when building JSON.

checks.message: Avoid direct string concatenation when constructing JSON. Use secure JSON libraries instead.
